title: Migrate Python dependency management to Poetry for Snyk OSS scanning
description: Switch python/ from hatch+pypi requirements duplication to Poetry (pyproject + poetry.lock) as single source of truth for dependency scanning. Remove requirements files/workflow, update CI/release/docs, verify make check and Snyk CLI detection.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text:
Starting implementation on branch . Plan: migrate  to Poetry ( backend), generate , remove  and requirements-sync workflow, update CI/release/docs, run , cd rust && cargo build
cd python && python -m black --check .
cd python && python -m ruff check .
All checks passed!
cd python && VIRTUUS_BACKEND=python python -m coverage run -m behave --exclude benchmarks

  Scenario: Create a hash+range GSI                                                  # ../features/gsi/gsi_creation.feature:11

    And the GSI should have sort key "created_at"                                    # ../features/steps/gsi_steps.py:207

  Scenario: Create multiple GSIs on the same table          # ../features/gsi/gsi_creation.feature:16

Feature: Referential integrity under load # ../features/concurrency/referential_integrity_under_load.feature:1

    And 100 posts referencing 50 users                              # ../features/steps/concurrency_steps.py:724
    When 5 threads continuously delete random users                 # ../features/steps/concurrency_steps.py:741
    And 20 threads continuously resolve post author associations    # ../features/steps/concurrency_steps.py:814

    And no thread should crash                                      # ../features/steps/concurrency_steps.py:853

  Scenario: Validate during concurrent mutations                      # ../features/concurrency/referential_integrity_under_load.feature:22
    Given a database with "users" and "posts" tables                  # ../features/steps/database_steps.py:179

    And a thread calls db.validate()                                  # ../features/steps/concurrency_steps.py:875
